scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a standard player-account and withdrawal-status request only when the account, wallet, KYC, and case records aligned.
Missing player, property, and transaction prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative player servicing.
Unsupported requests to override exclusion and bonus rules were treated as approval-bound exceptions and routed rather than executed in-lane.
A player-service conversation that turned into AML-sensitive review was handed to the correct owner without disclosing internal posture.
Repeat loyalty and bonus-wallet failures triggered a cross-property escalation package instead of another generic status response.
Settled-wager regrade requests were routed to sportsbook settlement or compliance instead of being over-resolved by player services.
The lane rejected generic VIP support framing and re-established gaming-specific evidence requirements.
Conflicting house-rule and tenant-policy handling triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis.
Unsupported third-party withdrawal and bonus-transfer requests were rejected cleanly and treated as boundary-sensitive cash or compliance issues.
Tenant-specific withdrawal and exclusion gates were applied as stricter overlays without losing the public gaming-control baseline.
The in-scope player-services scenario preserved gaming terminology such as player card, tier points, bonus wallet, TITO ticket, and cage follow-up instead of collapsing into generic support wording.
Missing jurisdiction, exclusion status, and contradictory balance evidence triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside player services and does not claim sportsbook adjudication, cage approval, AML, surveillance, legal, or discretionary exception authority.
